<content>1: # Access Code Gate (Coupon Logic) - Specification
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: To provide specific institutions (e.g., schools) with free access to paid or restricted courses/lessons via a "Coupon Code" mechanism. This allows the system to grant access without traditional payment while enabling the administration to track exactly which students (by name and email) are utilizing each school's allocation.
6: 
7: ## Requirements
8: 
9: ### FR-001: Add Access Code Gate Access Type
10: 
11: **Priority**: MUST
12: **Description**: Add a new `accessCode` option to the access type system that can be set on courses and lessons. When content has this access type, it triggers the code gate UI instead of the existing gated/mandatory flows.
13: 
14: **Implementation Notes**:
15: - Add `accessCode` to `AccessType` and `LessonAccessType` in `@/infra/auth/access-types.ts`
16: - The frontend checks access type and shows code gate modal when `accessCode`
17: 
18: ### FR-002: Access Codes Collection
19: 
20: **Priority**: MUST
21: **Description**: Create a new `access-codes` Payload collection for storing generated access codes.
22: 
23: **Fields**:
24: - `code`: text (unique) - The code string (e.g., "MACCABI-2024-FREE")
25: - `scopeType`: select - Scope of unlock: `lesson`, `course`, `global`
26: - `scopeTarget`: relationship - Points to specific lesson or course (conditional on scopeType)
27: - `maxRedemptions`: number (optional) - Maximum uses allowed
28: - `currentRedemptions`: number - Auto-incremented on use
29: - `isActive`: checkbox - Whether code is currently valid
30: - `expiresAt`: date (optional) - Expiration date
31: - `createdBy`: relationship to users - Admin who created the code
32: 
33: ### FR-003: User Redeemed Codes Field
34: 
35: **Priority**: MUST
36: **Description**: Add field to Users collection to track which content items the user has unlocked via codes.
37: 
38: **Fields**:
39: - `redeemedAccessCodes`: array of objects
40:   - `codeId`: relationship to access-codes
41:   - `contentId`: text (stores lesson/course ID that was unlocked)
42:   - `contentType`: select (`lesson` | `course`)
43:   - `redeemedAt`: date
44: 
45: ### FR-004: Code Redemptions Collection
46: 
47: **Priority**: MUST
48: **Description**: Create `code-redemptions` collection for audit trail.
49: 
50: **Fields**:
51: - `code`: relationship to access-codes
52: - `user`: relationship to users
53: - `contentId`: text
54: - `contentType`: select
55: - `redeemedAt`: date
56: 
57: ### FR-005: Code Redemption API
58: 
59: **Priority**: MUST
60: **Description**: Create API endpoint for validating and redeeming access codes.
61: 
62: **Endpoint**: `POST /api/access-codes/redeem`
63: **Request Body**: `{ code: string, contentId: string, contentType: string }`
64: **Response**: `{ success: boolean, message: string, unlockedContent?: {...} }`
65: 
66: **Validation Logic**:
67: 1. Check user is authenticated
68: 2. Find code in access-codes collection
69: 3. Verify code is active and not expired
70: 4. Check maxRedemptions not reached (if set)
71: 5. Check user hasn't already redeemed this code for this content
72: 6. Increment code's currentRedemptions
73: 7. Add to user's redeemedAccessCodes
74: 8. Create code-redemption record
75: 9. Return success
76: 
77: ### FR-006: Access Code Gate Modal Component
78: 
79: **Priority**: MUST
80: **Description**: Create frontend component for code entry popup.
81: 
82: **Implementation**:
83: - New component: `src/ui/web/auth/AccessCodeGateModal.tsx`
84: - Uses existing Dialog component from design system
85: - Shows blur effect on content behind modal
86: - Input field for code entry
87: - Submit button "Unlock"
88: - Error state: "Incorrect code. Please check with your teacher."
89: - Success: Closes modal, reveals content, stores redemption
90: 
91: ### FR-007: Admin Usage Report Export
92: 
93: **Priority**: SHOULD
94: **Description**: Allow admins to export redemption data as CSV.
95: 
96: **Implementation**:
97: - Add export button to access code detail view
98: - Generate CSV with columns: Student Name, Email, Date Redeemed
99: - Use existing endpoint pattern for file download
100: 
101: ### NFR-001: Translation Strings
102: 
103: **Priority**: MUST
104: **Description**: Add Hebrew and English translations for code gate UI.
105: 
106: **Strings Required**:
107: - `accessCodeTitle`: "Access Restricted"
108: - `accessCodeDescription`: "Please insert your school access code to unlock this content."
109: - `accessCodePlaceholder`: "Enter code"
110: - `accessCodeUnlock`: "Unlock"
111: - `accessCodeError`: "Incorrect code. Please check with your teacher."
112: - `accessCodeSuccess`: "Content unlocked!"
113: 
114: ### NFR-002: Access Control Security
115: 
116: **Priority**: MUST
117: **Description**: Ensure code redemption is secure and cannot be exploited.
118: 
119: **Requirements**:
120: - Code validation must happen server-side
121: - Rate limiting on redemption endpoint to prevent brute force
122: - Admin-only access to create/modify codes
123: - Users can only read their own redemption history
124: 
125: ## Acceptance Criteria
126: 
127: - [ ] A student from "School A" can log in, enter a code, and immediately see their lesson
128: - [ ] The Admin can go to the dashboard and see that "Student Name (Email)" redeemed the "School A" code at 10:00 AM
129: - [ ] Content that is not flagged remains open and accessible as usual
130: - [ ] Invalid codes show error message and do not grant access
131: - [ ] Students who redeemed a code are never asked for the code again for that content
132: - [ ] Admins can create codes with lesson, course, or global scope
133: - [ ] Admins can set optional max redemption limits
134: - [ ] Admins can export redemption data as CSV
135: 
136: ## Guardrails
137: 
138: - **Existing Access Types**: Must not break existing `free`, `mandatory`, `gated` access types
139: - **User Data**: Must not expose user email/name to unauthorized parties
140: - **Code Security**: Must prevent brute force attacks on code redemption
141: - **Performance**: Must not add significant latency to content loading
142: 
143: ## Out of Scope
144: 
145: - Integration with payment systems (this is a free access mechanism, not paid)
146: - Multi-tenant code management (single tenant assumed)
147: - Time-based access (codes are valid until manually deactivated or max redemptions reached)
148: - Student self-service code generation

(End of file - total 148 lines)
</content>

# Access Code Gate (Coupon Logic) - Specification

## Overview

To provide specific institutions (e.g., schools) with free access to paid or restricted courses/lessons via a "Coupon Code" mechanism. This allows the system to grant access without traditional payment while enabling the administration to track exactly which students (by name and email) are utilizing each school's allocation.

## Requirements

### FR-001: Add Access Code Gate Access Type

**Priority**: MUST
**Description**: Add a new `accessCode` option to the access type system that can be set on courses and lessons. When content has this access type, it triggers the code gate UI instead of the existing gated/mandatory flows.

**Implementation Notes**:
- Add `accessCode` to `AccessType` and `LessonAccessType` in `@/infra/auth/access-types.ts`
- The frontend checks access type and shows code gate modal when `accessCode`

### FR-002: Access Codes Collection

**Priority**: MUST

# Plan: Access Code Gate (Coupon Logic)

## Rerun Context

This is a rerun requested via `/cody rerun`. No previous plan/build/review artifacts exist. This is a fresh plan based on the spec + clarified answers.

**Clarified answers applied**:
1. Same code for multiple students → Multi-use codes (one code, many students)
2. Lesson only → Scope is limited to individual lessons (not course-level or global)
3. No → If code is deleted, students who redeemed do NOT retain access
4. Yes → Codes can have time-based expiration

**Spec Deviations (per clarified answers):**
- **FR-002 scope narrowed**: Spec says `scopeType` MUST support `lesson`, `course`, `global`. Per clarified answer #2, this plan implements `lesson` scope only. The `scopeType` field is extensible for future course/global support.
- **FR-003 replaced by FR-004**: Spec says MUST add `redeemedAccessCodes` array to Users. This plan uses the `code-redemptions` collection (FR-004) + a check endpoint instead, avoiding unbounded arrays on User documents. Same persistence guarantee, better data architecture.

## Research Findings

**File paths verified:**
- ✅ `src/infra/auth/access-types.ts` — Current access types: `free`, `mandatory`, `gated`

- `tests/unit/i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts` - Unit tests for i18n translations (NFR-001)
- `tests/int/access-codes.int.spec.ts` - Integration tests for access codes feature

## Test Files

| File | Test Count | Type |
|------|-----------|------|
| tests/unit/access-types.test.ts | 11 | unit |
| tests/unit/i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts | 20 | unit |
| tests/int/access-codes.int.spec.ts | 12 | integration |

## Test Cases

| Test Name | Type | Expected Behavior |
|-----------|------|-------------------|
| ACCESS_TYPES includes accessCode | unit | Verifies 'accessCode' is in the ACCESS_TYPES constant |
| LESSON_ACCESS_TYPES includes accessCode | unit | Verifies 'accessCode' is in the LESSON_ACCESS_TYPES constant |
| resolveAccessType returns accessCode for lesson | unit | Verifies resolveAccessType('accessCode', 'free') returns 'accessCode' |
| resolveAccessType returns accessCode for course | unit | Verifies resolveAccessType('inherit', 'accessCode') returns 'accessCode' |
| en.json contains accessCode translations | unit | Verifies all 8 translation keys exist in English |
| he.json contains accessCode translations | unit | Verifies all 8 translation keys exist in Hebrew |
| existing translations not broken | unit | Verifies existing accessControl keys are preserved |
| can create lesson with accessType=accessCode | integration | Creates a lesson with accessType='accessCode' - MUST FAIL until Step 2 |
| can create course with accessType=accessCode | integration | Creates a course with accessType='accessCode' - MUST FAIL until Step 2 |
| existing access types still work | integration | Creates lesson with accessType='free' - should pass |
| admin can create access codes | integration | Admin creates access code - MUST FAIL until Step 3 |
| access code must be unique | integration | Duplicate code string should throw - MUST FAIL until Step 3 |
| non-admin cannot create codes | integration | Student user cannot create codes - MUST FAIL until Step 3 |
| code redemption can be created | integration | Creates a code redemption record - MUST FAIL until Step 4 |
| student can only read own redemptions | integration | Verifies user-scoped read access - MUST FAIL until Step 4 |
| admin can read all redemptions | integration | Admin sees all redemptions - MUST FAIL until Step 4 |
| redeem valid code | integration | Validates code redemption flow - MUST FAIL until Step 5 |
| unauthenticated cannot redeem | integration | Anonymous user denied - MUST FAIL until Step 5 |
| check returns redeemed status | integration | Query redemption status - MUST FAIL until Step 9 |
| admin can query redemptions | integration | Admin export query - MUST FAIL until Step 10 |

## Notes

- The unit tests in `tests/unit/access-types.test.ts` will FAIL because FR-001 is not implemented yet (accessCode type doesn't exist in ACCESS_TYPES)
- The unit tests in `tests/unit/i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts` will FAIL because NFR-001 translations haven't been added yet
- The integration tests will FAIL at various steps until the implementation is complete:
  - Steps 2-3 require accessCode type in collections (FR-001)
  - Steps 3-4 require AccessCodes and CodeRedemptions collections (FR-002, FR-004)
  - Steps 5, 9, 10 require API endpoints (FR-005)

```bash

pnpm test:unit tests/unit/i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts

# All access-codes tests
pnpm test:unit tests/unit/access-types.test.ts
pnpm test:unit tests/unit/i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts
pnpm test:int tests/int/access-codes.int.spec.ts
```

1. **access-types.test.ts** - `accessCode` not in ACCESS_TYPES constant
2. **i18n-access-code-keys.test.ts** - Translation keys don't exist
3. **access-codes.int.spec.ts** - Collections and types don't exist yet
